description: Trigger discrimination: whole-estate PII lifecycle (this skill) vs AI-boundary redaction (sensitive-disclosure-guard), audit-record retention (audit-log-architect), PII-safe test data (test-data-architect), and compliance evidence/control mapping (compliance-control-foundation).

cases.prompt: Map where personal data lives across our databases, warehouse, logs, and vendors, set retention per class, and design how erasure requests propagate — including what we tell users about backups.
cases.reason: Classification, the per-store data map, retention schedules, and propagating erasure are the lifecycle core. No AI boundary, audit schema, or auditor evidence packaging is requested.

cases.prompt: Audit what our LLM feature puts into prompts and logs, and design the data-minimization and redaction pipeline in front of the model.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — same nouns (PII, redaction, minimization), different boundary: pre-model context assembly and model I/O is the disclosure guard's pipeline (OWASP LLM02). The lifecycle designer's classification is an INPUT to it; the estate-wide retention/erasure design is not being asked for.
cases.reason: The subject is what enters model context and its logs — the AI boundary — not where data lives estate-wide or how it dies.

cases.prompt: Define retention and redaction rules for our audit trail — admin actions, data exports, security events — and who may read it.
cases.reason: The audit trail's schema, integrity, retention, and access are audit-log-architect's own design surface. The lifecycle map lists the audit log as one more store and consumes that design — it does not author it.

cases.prompt: QA keeps copying production user data into staging for tests. Replace that with safe synthetic fixtures or an approved anonymization path.
cases.reason: PII-safe TEST data — synthetic generation and the anonymization path for fixtures — is test-data-architect's explicit scope. The lifecycle designer governs production personal data, not test-fixture strategy.

cases.prompt: For the upcoming audit, map our data-protection controls to the framework criteria and assemble the evidence showing retention and deletion actually run.
cases.reason: Control catalogs, criteria mapping, and evidence packaging are the compliance pack's projection work. The lifecycle design is the implementing mechanism those controls CITE — producing auditor-grade artifacts is not its job.
